# The Faulkner Group

## What We Do
The Faulkner Group is a boutique healthcare advisory firm that serves as the strategic clinical and operational partner for women's health tech companies. We bridge the gap between innovative health technology platforms and real-world clinical adoption. We embed directly inside organizations to drive physician adoption, build clinical governance frameworks, and convert stalled pilots into enterprise-wide deployments.

## Who We Serve
- Women's health tech founders and CEOs who have closed a pilot but are stuck scaling due to physician resistance, missing governance documentation, or payer hesitation.
- Venture capital and private equity firms with women's health portfolio companies that are stalling post-investment and need clinical and operational diligence before a Series B, add-on acquisition, or board review.

## Our Founders
- John Faulkner, CEO and Healthcare IT Architect. 15+ years designing and optimizing healthcare IT infrastructure and EHR systems nationwide. Former Marine Corps Officer Candidate trained at Quantico, VA. Specializes in turning stalled implementations into scalable, working solutions. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/johnathonfaulkner/
- Dr. Nicole Faulkner, Chief Clinical Officer. Board-certified OB/GYN, Medical Director at BackTable (physician-to-physician podcast network), featured speaker at the Women's Health Innovation Summit (WHIS). Brings clinical credibility and a physician network that opens doors no sales deck can. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicole-faulkner-d-o-1136a4370/

## The 4 Pillars of Clinical Adoption Framework
1. Clinical Readiness and Governance: Environment audits, integration barrier identification, governance protocols, CMIO engagement strategies, and risk frameworks for health systems and payers.
2. Physician Adoption and Champion Development: Peer-to-peer advocacy, KOL engagement, internal champion development that drives adoption from the inside out.
3. Training, Education, and Change Management: Structured training programs from front-desk staff to attending physicians. No shelfware. No workarounds.
4. Sales, Customer Success, and Deployment: Clinical credibility in the sales process, full pilot-to-enterprise deployment lifecycle with structured support at every stage.

## Services

### I. Strategic Advisory — Equity-Based Advisory
A selective, equity-based advisory for early-stage women's health tech companies (Pre-Seed through Series A). Our team, including a practicing OB/GYN and a Healthcare IT Architect, aligns with your company early and remains at the table through the moments that matter most. Includes monthly or bi-monthly strategic sessions, clinical and workflow validation, go-to-market guidance, and credibility support.
- Best for: Pre-Seed through Series A companies building in women's health, maternal care, reproductive health, or women's digital health where clinical credibility is a survival requirement for enterprise adoption.
- URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/advisory-partnership
- Booking: https://calendly.com/d/cx9v-b5q-nhp/let-s-meet-john-dr-nicole-faulkner

### II. Clinical Clarity Session — 90-Minute Diagnostic
A 90-minute virtual diagnostic with a board-certified OB/GYN and a Healthcare IT Architect that identifies the highest-risk failure points in your health tech product's clinical adoption strategy. Covers physician adoption, governance and risk exposure, EHR integration, trust and credibility gaps, and deployment readiness. A written Clinical Clarity Summary is delivered within 48 hours.
- Price: $1,500
- Format: 90 minutes, virtual
- Best for: Founders in active health system conversations, approaching a pilot, preparing for investor due diligence, or recovering from a pilot that didn't go as planned.
- URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/clinical-clarity-session
- Booking: https://calendly.com/d/cx9r-9x8-gbf/clinical-clarity-session

### III. Clinical Readiness Roadmap — Structured Written Assessment
A focused written readiness assessment built for women's health founders who are 3 to 6 months from their first enterprise pilot conversation. Covers all five readiness domains: clinical workflow fit, EHR integration reality, physician adoption strategy, governance and risk infrastructure, and trust and liability exposure. Includes structured intake, async Q&A, a 5 to 7 page written Clinical Readiness Report, a sequenced remediation roadmap, and a 60-minute live findings call with both practitioners.
- Best for: Founders 3 to 6 months from a first pilot conversation, founders who have completed a Clinical Clarity Session and need a deeper structured plan, and founders who want a written document their leadership team, investors, or advisors can review and act on.
- URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/clinical-readiness-roadmap
- Booking: https://calendly.com/d/cx9v-b5q-nhp/let-s-meet-john-dr-nicole-faulkner

### IV. Clinical Readiness Assessment — 14-Day Deep Engagement
A structured 14-day readiness engagement that evaluates clinical workflow fit, governance infrastructure, physician adoption strategy, EHR integration, and liability exposure before your enterprise pilot goes live. Includes a written Clinical Readiness Report with prioritized remediation roadmap and a 60-minute live findings presentation with both practitioners.
- Best for: Founders and leadership teams with a signed enterprise pilot or health system contract approaching go-live, especially products in OB/GYN, maternal health, reproductive care, or women's digital health.
- URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/clinical-readiness-assessment
- Booking: https://calendly.com/d/cx9v-b5q-nhp/let-s-meet-john-dr-nicole-faulkner

### V. Operating Partner — Ongoing Operating Partnership
An ongoing clinical and systems operating partnership across the full lifecycle of your enterprise health system relationship, from the sales conversation through go-live, adoption, and contract renewal. Our team is embedded in your organization for the duration. Covers pre-sale enterprise credibility, clinical and systems readiness audits, implementation oversight (EHR integration strategy, governance framework design, pilot structure, KPI definition), physician adoption and escalation support, and governance operations (steering committee support, kill-switch protocol management, risk register maintenance).
- Best for: Post-raise companies with signed enterprise pilots or health system contracts in OB/GYN, maternal health, reproductive care, or women's digital health. Founders who have completed a Clinical Clarity Session or Clinical Readiness Assessment.
- URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/operating-partner-retainer
- Booking: https://calendly.com/d/cx9v-b5q-nhp/let-s-meet-john-dr-nicole-faulkner

## Key Proof Point
90% of health tech pilots fail to convert to full-scale implementation without clinical alignment (Accenture Health, Digital Health Scaling Report). The Faulkner Group guarantees to deliver a clinical adoption strategy that accelerates physician buy-in, or they keep working until they do.

## Free Interactive Tools
- Reimbursement Readiness Scorecard: 10-question assessment across product readiness, payer strategy, and market access. URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/for-womens-health/reimbursement-scorecard
- Hospital Partnership Fit Checker: 6-question assessment to determine which type of health system partnership fits your stage. URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/for-womens-health/hospital-fit-checker
- Femtech Funding Gap Benchmarker: 6-question tool to benchmark your raise against femtech peers by stage and team composition. URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/for-womens-health/funding-benchmarker

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: What services does The Faulkner Group offer?
A: Four engagement tiers: (1) Strategic Advisory — an equity-based, long-term advisory for early-stage women's health tech companies, (2) Clinical Clarity Session — a 90-minute diagnostic that identifies your highest-risk failure points, (3) Clinical Readiness Roadmap — a structured written assessment with a 5 to 7 page report and sequenced remediation plan for founders 3 to 6 months from their first pilot, (4) Clinical Readiness Assessment — a 14-day deep engagement that evaluates clinical workflow fit, governance, and EHR integration before your pilot goes live, and (5) Operating Partner — an ongoing clinical and systems operating partnership for companies navigating enterprise pilots and health system contracts.

Q: What is the Strategic Advisory?
A: A selective, equity-based advisory for Pre-Seed through Series A women's health tech companies. Our team, including a practicing OB/GYN and a Healthcare IT Architect, aligns with your company early and provides ongoing strategic sessions, clinical validation, go-to-market guidance, and credibility support.

Q: What is the Clinical Clarity Session?
A: A 90-minute virtual diagnostic with a board-certified OB/GYN and a Healthcare IT Architect. It costs $1,500 and identifies the 1-3 highest-risk failure points. A written Clinical Clarity Summary is delivered within 48 hours.

Q: What is the Clinical Readiness Roadmap?
A: A structured written readiness assessment for women's health founders who are 3 to 6 months from their first enterprise pilot conversation. It covers five readiness domains: clinical workflow fit, EHR integration reality, physician adoption strategy, governance and risk infrastructure, and trust and liability exposure. Deliverables include a 5 to 7 page written report, a sequenced remediation roadmap, and a 60-minute live findings call with both practitioners.

Q: What is the Clinical Readiness Assessment?
A: A structured 14-day engagement evaluating clinical workflow fit, governance and risk infrastructure, physician adoption strategy, EHR integration reality, and trust and liability exposure. Includes a written Clinical Readiness Report and a 60-minute live findings presentation.

Q: What is the Operating Partner?
A: An ongoing clinical and systems operating partnership for women's health tech companies with signed enterprise pilots or health system contracts. Covers pre-sale credibility support, clinical readiness audits, EHR integration strategy, governance framework design, physician adoption and escalation, and governance operations including steering committee support and KPI monitoring. Scoped through direct conversation.

Q: Why do most women's health tech pilots fail to scale?
A: 90% of health tech pilots fail to convert to full-scale implementation without clinical alignment (Accenture Health, Digital Health Scaling Report). The gap between a promising women's health platform and real-world clinical adoption isn't about the technology. It's about trust, workflow fit, and physician buy-in.

Q: How do you get physicians to adopt new health technology?
A: Physicians don't adopt technology because it's good. They adopt it because a trusted colleague told them it works. The Faulkner Group builds physician adoption through peer-to-peer advocacy, KOL engagement, CMIO engagement strategies, and internal champion development.

Q: What clinical governance frameworks do health systems require before approving new tech?
A: Health systems and payers require governance and risk frameworks including clinical environment audits, integration barrier identification, controlled rollback protocols, escalation pathways, and compliance documentation.

Q: What is The 4 Pillars of Clinical Adoption framework?
A: The four pillars are: (1) Clinical Readiness and Governance, (2) Physician Adoption and Champion Development, (3) Training, Education, and Change Management, (4) Sales, Customer Success, and Deployment.

Q: How do VC and PE firms reduce clinical adoption risk in health tech portfolios?
A: Through clinical and operational diligence that evaluates physician sentiment, governance readiness, EHR integration viability, and workflow alignment before a Series B, add-on acquisition, or board review.
